name: Sugar apple / sweetsop (Annona squamosa) (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw edible pulp). concentration_mM assumes 100 g ~ 100 mL (water-rich matrix, density ~1).

components.name: water
components.source: USDA FDC 168175 (SR Legacy, Sugar-apples, (sweetsop), raw; NDB 09520)
components.evidence: matrix / solvent, ~73% w/w

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Kumar et al. review (Foods, MDPI 14(19):3413 / PMC12523297; total sugars ~18.15%) + Yang/Paull postharvest review (S0963996911001037, reducing sugars glucose+fructose dominate over sucrose)
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon source; free sugars are ~starch-hydrolysis products. Amount is a literature-derived split of ~18 g/100g total sugars (glucose ~= fructose, reducing sugars > sucrose); treat as approximate (range for total sugars 15.6-18.2 g/100g across cultivars)

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: Kumar et al. review PMC12523297 / postharvest review S0963996911001037 (glucose:fructose ~50:50 reducing-sugar pool)
components.evidence: co-dominant carbon source with glucose; approximate literature-derived split (see D-glucose note)

components.name: sucrose
components.source: Kumar et al. review PMC12523297 (non-reducing sugar, secondary to reducing sugars; sucrose rises on ripening)
components.evidence: secondary carbon source; approximate literature-derived split (see D-glucose note)

components.name: total sugars
components.source: Kumar et al. review PMC12523297 (18.15%); cultivar range 15.6-18.2 g/100g
components.evidence: aggregate of the free sugars above; provided for reconciliation, not a separate consumable

components.name: starch
components.source: Postharvest review S0963996911001037 (sugars are starch-hydrolysis products; starch declines as fruit ripens)
components.evidence: present in less-ripe pulp, near-zero at eating-ripe stage; unquantified

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Yang/Paull postharvest review S0963996911001037 + Kumar review PMC12523297 (citric is the predominant acid); titratable acidity of ripe pulp ~0.29 g/100g as citric-equiv
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid and a secondary carbon/energy source; was ABSENT from the FooDB profile. Amount apportioned from ripe-pulp titratable acidity (~0.29 g/100g, citric>>malic>isocitric); approximate

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Postharvest review S0963996911001037 (malic present at low content, second to citric)
components.evidence: secondary organic acid; was absent from FooDB profile; approximate from titratable-acidity apportionment

components.name: isocitric acid
components.evidence: genuine minor acid, present but not separately quantified

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: modest-protein fruit; higher than most fleshy fruits

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: one of the few USDA-quantified free/bound amino acids for this fruit

components.name: other protein-bound amino acids
components.source: USDA FDC 168175 (only Lys/Trp/Met quantified); full profile not measured
components.evidence: remaining ~1.98 g/100g protein present as unresolved amino acids (Arg/Glu/Asp-rich by analogy to Annona seed profiles); unquantified

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: principal mineral

components.name: iron
components.evidence: single consolidated iron pool; FooDB listed 3 compartmental duplicates (Iron, Iron(Fe3+), Fe2+ mitochondria) - collapsed to one here

components.name: manganese
components.source: Kumar review PMC12523297 (Mn 0.21-1.75 mg/100g, midpoint used)
components.evidence: USDA panel did not quantify; review-derived midpoint

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: trace

components.name: molybdenum (as molybdate)
components.evidence: genuine trace micronutrient, unquantified; retained

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: also functions as an organic acid; high in this fruit; listed once here

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.source: Kumar review PMC12523297 (B-complex present); not quantified
components.evidence: trace, unquantified

components.name: vitamin B12
components.evidence: absent (plant food); flag if a model requires B12 supplementation

components.name: beta-carotene (provitamin A)
components.source: Kumar review PMC12523297 (vitamin A activity 1.8-7.0 mcg RAE/100g; USDA FDC 168175 reports 0 RAE)
components.evidence: only a trace provitamin-A carotenoid; genuine but near-negligible, unquantified

components.name: total lipid (fat)
components.evidence: very low-fat matrix

components.name: monounsaturated fatty acids
components.evidence: dominant lipid fraction (largely oleic 18:1); pulp lipids negligible for growth

components.name: saturated fatty acids
components.evidence: largely palmitic 16:0 / stearic 18:0

components.name: polyunsaturated fatty acids
components.evidence: largely linoleic 18:2

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: partly fermentable by fiber-degrading microbes

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: preformed (animal-form) vitamin A; does not occur in plant fruit - only provitamin-A carotenoids do. USDA reports 0 RAE for this fruit; FooDB artifact

remove.name: Co2+ / Cobalt
remove.reason: ultratrace, non-nutritive at fruit levels (no cobalamin synthesis in a plant food); FooDB noise, mirrors handling in other plant-food curations

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: ultratrace non-nutritive element, not a growth-relevant nutrient at food concentrations; FooDB noise

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+) / Fe2+ mitochondria (duplicate iron entries)
remove.reason: compartmental BiGG duplicates of the same iron pool; consolidated into a single 'iron' 0.6 mg/100g component

missing_added: citric acid (~0.20 g/100g - DOMINANT organic acid, was completely absent from FooDB profile)
malic acid (~0.06 g/100g - secondary organic acid, was absent)
quantified individual sugars: D-glucose ~7.0, D-fructose ~6.0, sucrose ~5.0 g/100g (FooDB carried only qualitative 'Sucrose' and 'beta-D-Glucopyranose')
quantified full USDA vitamin/mineral panel amounts (C, B1/B2/B3/B5/B6/B9, K, Ca, Mg, P, Na, Fe, Zn, Cu, Se) plus review-derived Mn
amino-acid amounts (Lys 55, Trp 10, Met 7 mg/100g) and total protein 2.06 g
fatty-acid fraction breakdown and total fat
keep_uncovered: Boron (genuine ultratrace micronutrient; already uncovered, retained)
annonaceous acetogenins (squamocin, bullatacin, etc. - Annona-defining phytochemicals, no BiGG met)
flavonoids/polyphenols: rutin, quercetin, kaempferol, catechin, epicatechin, chlorogenic acid, gallic acid, caffeic/ferulic acids (genuine, no BiGG met)
ent-kaurenoic acid and related diterpenoids (genuine Annona phytochemicals)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 168175 (SR Legacy, Sugar-apples, (sweetsop), raw; NDB 09520) - proximates, vitamins, minerals, fatty-acid fractions, Lys/Trp/Met
Kumar et al., A Review of Nutrition, Bioactivities and Health Benefits of Custard Apple (Annona squamosa), Foods 2025, 14(19):3413 / PMC12523297 (total sugars ~18.15%, mineral & vitamin ranges, Mn)

Yang & Paull, Postharvest physiology and technology of Annona fruits, Food Res. Int. (S0963996911001037) - citric>>malic>isocitric acids; reducing sugars (glucose+fructose) dominate over sucrose; ripe-pulp titratable acidity ~0.29 g/100g
FooDB FOOD00485 (source of the original component list)
notes: Sugar apple is a SWEET, LOW-ACID fruit: the dominant carbon sources are the free reducing sugars glucose (~7) + fructose (~6) with secondary sucrose (~5 g/100g), ~18 g/100g total sugars. The dominant organic acid is CITRIC (~0.2 g/100g, > malic > isocitric) - completely missing from the original FooDB profile and the single most important gap fixed here. Individual sugar and organic-acid absolutes are literature-derived apportionments (total sugars 18.15%; ripe titratable acidity 0.29 g/100g) and should be treated as approximate; the USDA vitamin/mineral/proximate panel is high-confidence. K/citrate-buffered, modest-protein (2.06 g), very-low-fat medium; a metabolic model may need vitamin B12 supplemented (absent). Removed one implausible animal-form vitamer (retinol), two ultratrace non-nutritive metals (cobalt, nickel), and consolidated three duplicate iron entries.
